narrative: Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz dominates the weekend tape, setting up a geopolitical risk premium and crude-linked volatility for the Tokyo open. EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1469; the hawkish Fed debut of Governor Warsh and a +145bp OIS carry advantage likely to maintain downside pressure. USD/JPY closed Friday at 161.28; while geopolitical risk are likely to support the pair on USD demand, the BoJ's July hike bias and a positive CESI (+17) would limit upside if haven flows pivot to JPY. NZD/USD faces a critical test at the open as the G10 laggard (1W avg: -0.792%) against a dominant USD (1W avg: +1.097%).
